text.title: Figma AI Tools Overview
text.summary: Figma Make, Draw, Sites, and AI Image Tools — capabilities, hard limits, and plan requirements.

list.item: Generates web app prototypes from text prompts; collaborative editing with AI assistant
Direct connection to design system tokens (partial — not all variables are respected)
Limit: static placeholders only; no real data source connection
Limit: conditional logic is limited; not suitable for complex multi-state interactive prototyping
Limit: browser-only; no public API as of 2026

rule.statement: Use Figma Make for 10-minute prototype generation; always rebuild final UI in proper Figma components.
rule.rationale: Make output is for exploration and stakeholder alignment, not as a design deliverable or production starting point.

rule.statement: Keep Figma Make prompts under 150 words. The in-app AI context window is limited; longer prompts produce degraded output.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated Figma Make prompts often exceed the effective context window; brevity and specificity improve output quality.

list.item: Generative AI sketching for vector graphics assistance
Limit: generative output lacks precision of manual vector work; not suitable for production icon sets
Limit: no headless API; in-canvas generative sketching only

rule.statement: Keep Figma Draw sketches in a separate draft page. Do not mix generative vector sketches with component library frames.
rule.rationale: Mixing generative and production content in the same Figma pages creates ambiguity during handoff and audits.

list.item: Publishes static site directly from Figma for stakeholder previews
Limit: not a CMS; content changes require re-export from Figma
Limit: no server-side rendering; poor SEO for SEO-sensitive use cases

rule.statement: Publish Figma Sites only on a custom subdomain with a clear "prototype, not production" header.
rule.rationale: Stakeholders who receive a Figma Sites URL without context may treat it as the production site; explicit labeling prevents scope confusion.

list.item: Erase Objects — select and remove unwanted elements from canvas images
Isolate Objects — separate subjects for moving or applying effects
Expand Images — extend backgrounds for new formats
Advantage: works directly on canvas without export/re-import cycle
Limit: probabilistic; fails on complex textures, faces, and branded photography

rule.statement: Generate 3 variations when using AI image Expand for hero banners; single-run outputs are often asymmetric.
rule.rationale: AI expansion is probabilistic; single-run outputs frequently have visible seams or asymmetric fill. Selection from multiple variants is the reliable pattern.

rule.statement: Verify plan gating before designing workflows that depend on Figma AI features. All AI features require Figma Professional/Organization plans.
rule.rationale: Free/Starter plan users cannot access Make, Draw, Sites AI features, or AI image tools; workflows designed without plan verification will fail for a subset of teams.
